advisory: June 2026. The fleet runs tollbooth-dpyc 0.52.1. v0.52.0 decoupled vault_source (where the Neon URL comes from: env vs Authority bootstrap) from purchase_mode (direct | certified | auto), where 'auto' derives certify-up from the dpyc-community registry chain; this is breaking ONLY for callers that hardcoded purchase_mode='direct' (the Authorities, already updated). v0.52.1 parallelizes Nostr profile relay I/O so get_nostr_profile / publish_nostr_profile are bounded by the slowest single relay (~5s) instead of crawling the set sequentially. NETWORK MINIMUM is 0.49.0: operators below it read the legacy kind-4 bootstrap DM and cannot bootstrap against the current Authorities, which publish bootstrap config only as the NIP-33 kind-30078 replaceable event. HISTORICAL SECURITY FLOOR: v0.44.2 fixed a credential-card redemption leak (receive_credentials could echo raw values) — never run below it.
architecture_notes: Schema symmetry: every operator and Authority gets its own Neon schema; credentials live in an encrypted_blob column. vault_source and purchase_mode are independent axes (a sub-Authority self-provisions its Neon from env yet still certifies up). Each service reports its version in service_status from its own pyproject [project].version (installed metadata, with a from-source pyproject.toml fallback) — there is no hand-maintained version constant to drift. Standard tool bodies live in tested tollbooth.tools.* modules behind thin proof-gate shims; tool names and behavior are unchanged.
